title: Georgian Nightmare Non-Recognition Act
stage_label: Referred to the Committee on Foreign Affairs, and in addition to the Committee on the Judiciary, for a period to be subs
plain_summary: This bill says the United States will not recognize Bidzina Ivanishvili or any Georgian government that he leads. The US government cannot spend money or take actions that would show they accept Ivanishvili as a leader. Instead, the United States will only recognize the president of Georgia who was in office before the October 2024 elections as the real leader. The President can change this policy only if special government officials confirm that Georgia has held fair and honest elections.
last_action_text: Sponsor introductory remarks on measure. (CR H189)
subjects: Sovereignty, recognition, national governance and status

sections.title: Section 1 Short Title
sections.text: This law can be called the Georgian Nightmare Non-Recognition Act.

sections.title: Section 2 Prohibition of Recognition of Ivanishvili Regime
sections.text: The United States has made several official policies. First, the United States will not recognize or have normal relations with any government of Georgia that is led by Bidzina Ivanishvili or his representatives. This is because the Ivanishvili government has committed ongoing crimes against the Georgian people. Second, the United States will actively work against other countries recognizing or having normal relations with any Georgian government led by Bidzina Ivanishvili or his representatives. The United States will do this by fully using required main and secondary sanctions in Executive Order number fourteen thousand twenty four. Third, the United States will use all available powers to stop corrupt activities or foreign influence operations by Georgia on behalf of the Chinese Communist Party, the Iranian government, and the Russian Federation. These powers include those provided under the Global Magnitsky Act and Executive Order number fourteen thousand twenty four. Because of these policies, no federal official or employee may take any action to recognize Bidzina Ivanishvili or any government in Georgia led by him or his representatives. No federal money may be used to recognize them or suggest in any way that the United States recognizes them. The United States will recognize the person who was President of Georgia before the fake elections on October twenty sixth, two thousand twenty four, as the only legitimate leader in Georgia. If the Georgian constitution is restored, which would be shown by holding free and fair elections as confirmed by the Chairman and Co-Chairman of the U.S. Helsinki Commission (a group that monitors human rights), then the President can declare this policy void.
